steps.title: Read the vertex from the problem
steps.content: A maximum height of 277 feet occurring at $t = 3$ seconds means the parabola has its vertex at $(h, k) = (3, 277)$. Since this is a maximum, the parabola opens downward, so the leading coefficient $a$ is negative.

steps.title: Write vertex form
steps.content: Vertex form of a quadratic is $f(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$. Substituting $h = 3$ and $k = 277$ gives $f(t) = a(t - 3)^2 + 277$.

steps.title: Use the launch height to solve for $a$
Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $-16(t + 277)^2 - 3$ has vertex $(-277, -3)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • B) $-16(t - 277)^2 + 3$ has vertex $(277, 3)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • C) $-16(t + 3)^2 - 277$ has vertex $(-3, -277)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • D) $-16(t - 3)^2 + 277$ has vertex $(3, 277)$ and gives $f(0) = -144 + 277 = 133$. Keep.
That confirms D is correct.
